chunk_summary: This section discusses iron-folic acid (IFA) supplementation and anemia treatment pathways in India, focusing on standardized dosing schedules, delivery platforms, and practical strategies to improve adherence, particularly in stunting care and adolescent health.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of IFA supplementation in addressing anemia and supporting linear growth in children and adolescents.
Learn the standardized dosing schedules for different age groups and physiological states in India’s anemia programs.
Identify practical strategies to improve adherence to IFA supplementation and manage side effects.
Differentiate between prophylactic and therapeutic approaches to anemia treatment.
key_facts: Prophylactic IFA dosing includes biweekly syrup (20 mg iron + 100 mcg folic acid) for children 6–59 months, weekly tablets (45 mg iron + 400 mcg folic acid) for children 5–10 years, and weekly tablets (60 mg iron + 500 mcg folic acid) for adolescents 10–19 years.
Pregnant women and lactating mothers receive daily IFA supplementation starting in the second trimester and continuing postpartum.
Therapeutic anemia treatment requires alignment with local protocols, reassessment of hemoglobin response, and evaluation for non-adherence or underlying conditions if no improvement is observed.
Adolescent IFA adherence can be improved by linking supplementation to fatigue reduction, better school performance, and menstrual symptom relief.
Practical counseling includes advising iron intake with food to reduce gastrointestinal side effects and separating iron from calcium supplements when both are prescribed.
reference_quote: The AMB schedule includes prophylactic IFA for children 6–59 months as biweekly syrup (commonly 1 mL containing 20 mg elemental iron plus 100 mcg folic acid), for children 5–10 years as a weekly tablet (45 mg iron plus 400 mcg folic acid), and for adolescents 10–19 years as a weekly tablet (60 mg iron plus 500 mcg folic acid).
document_key_topics: Integration of anemia care in stunting prevention
